message.content:
You are confirmer #2, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it; for a code change the named regression test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if the fix were reverted). Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix G-018 in src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py
What: egress denylist scrubs the entire ANTHROPIC_* namespace + NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS/NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED.
Test(s): test_is_unsafe_subprocess_var (new params)
Steps: read the actual changed file(s); read the test and judge whether it discriminates; check no regression. For a doc/refactor item, judge correctness/coverage rather than revert-discrimination. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested). Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

diff --git a/src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py b/src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py
index 2b8808b..1d36cac 100644
--- a/src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py
+++ b/src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py
@@ -135,13 +135,14 @@ def is_metered_anthropic_credential(key: str) -> bool:
 # metered provider, defeating ADR-0003 / "no metered API". The claude CLI honours all of these.
 _UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS = frozenset(
     {
-        "ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL",
-        "ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL",
-        "ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_BASE_URL",
         "CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK",
         "CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX",
         "CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER",
         "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS",
+        # TLS-trust / egress-interception knobs the Node-based CLI honours: a custom CA or disabled
+        # cert verification enables a transparent off-box MITM proxy (pass 4, #G-018).
+        "NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS",
+        "NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED",
     }
 )
 
@@ -150,12 +151,16 @@ def is_unsafe_subprocess_var(key: str) -> bool:
     """True for any env var that must not reach the high-stakes subprocess (#101).
 
     Covers metered credentials, off-box/metered-provider redirects, proxies (which redirect
-    egress), and cloud-provider credentials (AWS_*/GOOGLE_*). The subscription OAuth token is
-    deliberately NOT matched.
+    egress), cloud-provider credentials (AWS_*/GOOGLE_*), and TLS-trust overrides. The ENTIRE
+    ``ANTHROPIC_*`` namespace is scrubbed (#G-018): any such var configures the SDK's endpoint /
+    auth / headers and could redirect off-box or onto a metered provider — the subscription token
+    is ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` (no ``ANTHROPIC_`` prefix) and is deliberately NOT matched.
     """
     ku = key.upper()
     return (
-        is_metered_anthropic_credential(key)
+        ku.startswith(
+            "ANTHROPIC_"
+        )  # whole namespace: endpoint/auth/headers/custom-headers (#G-018)
         or ku in _UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS
         or ku.endswith("_PROXY")  # HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY/ALL_PROXY/NO_PROXY (any case)
         or ku.startswith("AWS_")

100	HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT", "300")))
101	
102	MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_REASONING_RETRIES", "2")))
103	BACKOFF_BASE = max(0.0, float(_env("ASSAY_REASONING_BACKOFF", "1.5")))
104	RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = max(0.0, float(_env("ASSAY_RATELIMIT_BACKOFF", "30")))
105	RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_RATELIMIT_RETRIES", "5")))
106	# run_json re-rolls on a PARSE failure. This is independent of the transient/rate-limit retry
107	# budget above (which applies per generation) so the two do not multiply into a retry storm; an
108	# overall wall-clock deadline bounds the total regardless (#102).
109	JSON_REROLLS = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_JSON_REROLLS", "2")))
110	RUN_JSON_DEADLINE = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_RUN_JSON_DEADLINE", "600")))
111	
112	# OAuth subscription token name (external CLI contract — not renamed).
113	OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV = "CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN"
114	
115	
116	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
117	# Credential scrubbing (ADR-0003 / no metered API) — pure, forward-proof
118	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
119	def is_metered_anthropic_credential(key: str) -> bool:
120	    """True for any env var the Anthropic SDK would read as a METERED credential.
121	
122	    Matches the *shape* — any ``ANTHROPIC_*`` var naming a ``KEY`` or ``TOKEN`` — rather than
123	    a static list, so a newly-added metered var cannot silently slip through. The subscription
124	    ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` does not start with ``ANTHROPIC_`` and is deliberately kept.
125	
126	    The match is case-insensitive (pass 3, #F-003): a metered key set under a non-uppercase name
127	    (``anthropic_api_key``) must scrub too — the AWS_/GOOGLE_ checks already uppercase, so this
128	    closes the one credential path that did not.
129	    """
130	    ku = key.upper()
131	    return ku.startswith("ANTHROPIC_") and ("KEY" in ku or "TOKEN" in ku)
132	
133	
134	# Env vars that — beyond metered credentials — would redirect the subprocess OFF-BOX or onto a
135	# metered provider, defeating ADR-0003 / "no metered API". The claude CLI honours all of these.
136	_UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS = frozenset(
137	    {
138	        "CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK",
139	        "CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX",
140	        "CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER",
141	        "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS",
142	        # TLS-trust / egress-interception knobs the Node-based CLI honours: a custom CA or disabled
143	        # cert verification enables a transparent off-box MITM proxy (pass 4, #G-018).
144	        "NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS",
145	        "NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED",
146	    }
147	)
148	
149	
150	def is_unsafe_subprocess_var(key: str) -> bool:
151	    """True for any env var that must not reach the high-stakes subprocess (#101).
152	
153	    Covers metered credentials, off-box/metered-provider redirects, proxies (which redirect
154	    egress), cloud-provider credentials (AWS_*/GOOGLE_*), and TLS-trust overrides. The ENTIRE
155	    ``ANTHROPIC_*`` namespace is scrubbed (#G-018): any such var configures the SDK's endpoint /
156	    auth / headers and could redirect off-box or onto a metered provider — the subscription token
157	    is ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` (no ``ANTHROPIC_`` prefix) and is deliberately NOT matched.
158	    """
159	    ku = key.upper()
160	    return (
161	        ku.startswith(
162	            "ANTHROPIC_"
163	        )  # whole namespace: endpoint/auth/headers/custom-headers (#G-018)
164	        or ku in _UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS
165	        or ku.endswith("_PROXY")  # HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY/ALL_PROXY/NO_PROXY (any case)
166	        or ku.startswith("AWS_")
167	        or ku.startswith("GOOGLE_")
168	    )
169	
170	
171	def scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
172	    """The process env hardened for the high-stakes subprocess (ADR-0003 / no metered API).
173	
174	    Every unsafe var (:func:`is_unsafe_subprocess_var`) is **overwritten to empty**, not merely

38	        ("ANTHROPIC_FUTURE_TOKEN", True),  # forward-proofing

43	        ("claude_code_oauth_token", False),  # #F-003: lowercase subscription auth still kept

59	        ("CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX", True),

65	        ("ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_HEADERS", True),  # #G-018: whole ANTHROPIC_ namespace scrubbed
66	        ("anthropic_base_url", True),  # #G-018: lowercase namespace too
67	        ("NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS", True),  # #G-018: custom CA enables off-box MITM
68	        ("NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED", True),  # #G-018: disabling cert checks
69	        ("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", False),  # subscription auth — keep
70	        ("PATH", False),
71	        ("HOME", False),
72	    ],
73	)
74	def test_is_unsafe_subprocess_var(key, unsafe):
75	    assert is_unsafe_subprocess_var(key) is unsafe
76	
77	
78	def test_scrubbed_env_overwrites_unsafe_and_survives_sdk_merge(monkeypatch):
79	    # #101: the SDK builds the child env as {**os.environ, **options.env}, so omitting a key
80	    # leaks the inherited value. scrubbed_env must OVERWRITE unsafe vars to "" so the merge wins.
81	    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "[REDACTED]")
82	    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL", "http://attacker.example")

content.text: Now let me verify discrimination. The key new params for G-018: `ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_HEADERS`, `anthropic_base_url`, `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`, `NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED`. Let me confirm these would fail if the fix were reverted, and that the test passes now.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && (python3 -m pytest tests/test_reasoning.py::test_is_unsafe_subprocess_var -q 2>&1 | tail -15) || echo "trying venv"; ls .venv/bin/python* 2>/dev/null

content.text: Now the discrimination check: simulate reverting the fix and confirm the new params fail.

content.content:
ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_HEADERS -> old says unsafe: False (test expects True; discriminates: True )
anthropic_base_url -> old says unsafe: True (test expects True; discriminates: False )
NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS -> old says unsafe: False (test expects True; discriminates: True )
NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED -> old says unsafe: False (test expects True; discriminates: True )

content.text:
Confirmed. Under the reverted logic, `ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_HEADERS`, `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`, and `NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED` all return False but the test asserts True — so the test discriminates (fails on revert). `anthropic_base_url` happens to match old `_PROXY`/no — actually it returned True under old because... it doesn't, wait it shows True. Let me note: it matched because `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` upper is in UNSAFE_OLD. So that one param alone wouldn't discriminate, but the other three do. That's sufficient — reverting the fix breaks the test.

Let me verify no regression: full test file and that `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` (no ANTHROPIC_ prefix) is still kept.

All 70 pass. Now assessment of completeness/appropriateness:

- The fix scrubs the entire `ANTHROPIC_*` namespace (covering endpoint/auth/headers vars like `ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_HEADERS`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HEADERS`, future additions) plus the two Node TLS-trust knobs. This is broader and forward-proof versus the old static list. `is_metered_anthropic_credential` is subsumed by the `ANTHROPIC_` prefix check (still used independently elsewhere, so no dead code concern).
- `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` correctly preserved (no `ANTHROPIC_` prefix). `NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED` set to "" by scrubbed_env — empty string is not "0", so cert verification stays enabled (safe default). Case-insensitive matching preserved via `ku`.
- Idiomatic: frozenset, comment references, consistent with existing pattern.

One minor note: the test param `anthropic_base_url` does not independently discriminate (matched under old logic too), but three other new params do discriminate, so the test as a whole fails on revert. The fix is complete and the test discriminates.

input.reasoning:
Verified the fix in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py and test in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_reasoning.py.

APPROPRIATE: is_unsafe_subprocess_var now matches the entire ANTHROPIC_* namespace (ku.startswith("ANTHROPIC_")) — covering endpoint/auth/header redirect vars (ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_HEADERS, ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HEADERS, and any future ANTHROPIC_ var) — and adds NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS / NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED to _UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS to block off-box MITM via custom CA or disabled cert verification. This is strictly broader and more forward-proof than the prior static list, which the prefix check subsumes (is_metered_anthropic_credential remains used elsewhere, so no dead code). Subscription token CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN (no ANTHROPIC_ prefix) is correctly preserved and asserted False. Case-insensitivity preserved via ku=key.upper(). scrubbed_env overwrites to "" — for NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED "" is not "0" so cert checks remain on (safe). Idiomatic frozenset + clear comments.

COMPLETE / DISCRIMINATING: All 18 params of test_is_unsafe_subprocess_var pass; full file 70/70 pass. Simulated reverting to the prior logic: ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_HEADERS, NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS, and NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED all return False while the test asserts True — so the test FAILS on revert, proving it discriminates. (The param anthropic_base_url alone does not discriminate since the old static list already contained ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, but the other three new params make the test as a whole revert-sensitive.) Fully closes G-018.

content.text: Confirmed G-018. The fix is both appropriate and complete, and the test discriminates (fails if reverted).
